_doc: Acceptance test cases for SynthOverseer. Consumed by tools/overseer/verify.js. Each case fires a prompt via SynthRCON, tails the server log for the matching [pipeline #N] resolved line, then runs assertions on the extracted LLM response body + log trace. Pair with overseer-verification-prompts.md (the human-readable version).

_assertion_types.log_contains: raw log substring (any line). String value.
_assertion_types.log_contains_all: every string in `values[]` must appear in some log line.
_assertion_types.response_contains_any: extracted assistant content matches AT LEAST ONE of `values[]`. Case-insensitive.
_assertion_types.response_contains_all: extracted assistant content matches EVERY string in `values[]`. Case-insensitive.
_assertion_types.response_matches: extracted assistant content matches the regex in `value`.
_assertion_types.min_response_chars: extracted assistant content has at least `value` characters (catches empty/error replies).
_assertion_types.tool_called: named tool fired (via [tool-registry] log lines). Use for tools/2.6+.
_assertion_types.no_tool_called: no [tool-registry] log lines appeared (verifies non-tool path).

cases.prompt: I want better tools, what's first?
how much HP does a skeleton have?
cases._note: Until lookup-npc ships (2.7), the model should refuse to fabricate a stat and mention it lacks the tool. May be flaky depending on how strict the model is about deferring — if it fails, look at the response to decide if the primer needs tightening.
Special-cased command — no LLM call, just prints config. Verifier should expect command output, not LLM response.
